text:
Mukade (ムカデ, Mukade) is one of the Heavens Arena Floor Masters. He only appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 

Appearance

Mukade is tall and lanky, with tan skin and a bald head. When he and the other Floor Masters are under the influence of On in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission, his entire eyes are the bright yellow that only people's irises turn when affected by On, perhaps meaning that he has no sclera. 

His outfit consists of a burgundy skin-tight, full-body suit, seemingly made of latex or similar material, that zips from the stomach to the nose. He also wears several long, thin spikes over his arms, legs, and head to imitate the appearance of centipede legs. 

Plot

The Last Mission

Mukade first appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission at the start of the Battle Olympia as he walks into the arena at Heavens Arena with the other Floor Masters. 

He is later seen jumping rope incredibly fast in a training room with the other Floor Masters in preparation for the upcoming fights. Jed enters the training room and knocks out all of the Floor Masters, injecting them with his blood to put them under the influence of On. Mukade is left slumped over on the ropes of the boxing ring in the room, unconscious. 

When Gon and Killua eventually get trapped in a room with Mukade and eight of the other Floor Masters, Mukade goes to lunge at Killua but is swiftly knocked out by Killua's yoyo. Even after being knocked down, the Floor Masters all get back up and follow after Gon and Killua in a zombie-like state until the two boys manage to escape and leave the Floor Masters behind. 

Abilities & Powers

Not much is known about Mukade's abilities, even less so than some of the other Floor Masters. He is not shown to have a page in Killua's magazine detailing some of the Floor Masters' abilities. However, because he is a Floor Master, it can be assumed that Mukade is strong and proficient in Nen.

Mukade is incredibly fast while jumping rope, meaning he's quick on his feet and has excellent reflexes.

Trivia

Mukade means "centipede" in Japanese.
